hooks.command: (ruff format -- "$CLAUDE_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null || true; ruff check --fix -- "$CLAUDE_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null || true; python3 -m py_compile "$CLAUDE_FILE_PATH" 2>&1 || true) && bash ~/.claude/hooks/auto-fix-loop.sh

hooks.prompt: A research brief was just created. Respond with {"decision": "block", "reason": "Research complete. Tell the user: review the findings, then proceed with /plan or /full-pipeline. Architecture decisions must reference this research."}.

A feature brief was just created. Respond with {"decision": "block", "reason": "Feature brief created. Tell the user: run /design-doc {feature-name} to create the design document next. The feature name is the folder name inside docs/. Do not proceed until user acknowledges."}.

An implementation prompt file was just created. Respond with {"decision": "block", "reason": "Implementation prompt ready. Tell the user: run /implement-meta-prompt <path> to execute this prompt in a new session. The prompt is self-contained and can also be copy-pasted into any LLM. Do not proceed until user acknowledges."}.

A PRD or architecture doc was just written. Check if BOTH docs/*/prd.md AND docs/*/architecture.md now exist for this project. If both exist and no design-doc.md exists yet, respond with {"decision": "block", "reason": "PRD and architecture are both ready. Tell the user: run /design-doc to create the implementation-ready design document. Do not proceed until user acknowledges."}. If only one exists, respond with {"decision": "allow"}.

Before committing, verify: 1) Have tests been run and do they pass? 2) Has linting been run? 3) Is the commit message using conventional format (feat:, fix:, test:, etc.)? If tests or lint haven't been run in this session, respond with {"decision": "block", "reason": "Run tests and lint before committing."}. Otherwise {"decision": "allow"}.

hooks.command: echo '{"decision": "block", "reason": "Lock files should not be manually edited. Use the package manager."}'
echo 'CRITICAL CONTEXT TO PRESERVE: Review the current task list, key files being modified, and any in-progress decisions. Carry these forward after compaction.'

if [ -f ~/.claude/ghost-config.json ] && jq -e '.status | test("running|starting")' ~/.claude/ghost-config.json >/dev/null 2>&1; then bash ~/.claude/hooks/ghost-notify.sh phase "Claude Code needs attention"; else osascript -e 'display notification "Claude Code needs your attention" with title "Claude Code"'; fi

hooks.prompt: A subagent just finished. Verify: 1) Did it complete all assigned tasks? 2) If it wrote code, did it run tests? 3) Are there any TODO/FIXME left in files it modified? If incomplete, respond with {"decision": "block", "reason": "Subagent incomplete: [details]"}. Otherwise {"decision": "allow"}.

hooks.command: if [ -f ~/.claude/ghost-config.json ] && jq -e '.status | test("running|starting")' ~/.claude/ghost-config.json >/dev/null 2>&1; then echo '{"decision": "allow"}'; else osascript -e 'display notification "Claude finished" with title "Claude Code"' 2>/dev/null; fi

hooks.prompt:
Before stopping, verify ALL of the following:

1. TASK COMPLETION: All tasks from the task list are completed or explicitly deferred with a reason.
2. NO LOOSE ENDS: No TODO items remain in modified files without a linked issue.
3. TESTS RAN: If code was written or modified, tests were run and pass.
4. NO RATIONALIZATION: Check your last response — did you say 'we can address this later', 'this is good enough for now', 'we can defer this to a follow-up', or similar? If so, STOP. Either fix the issue now or explicitly flag it as a known limitation.
5. VERIFICATION: If implementation was done, were the verification commands run? (test, lint, typecheck)
6. AGENTS.MD: If you discovered a pattern, gotcha, or resolved a non-trivial error during this session, did you update AGENTS.md? If AGENTS.md exists and you learned something new, update it before stopping.

If anything is incomplete, respond with {"decision": "block", "reason": "Incomplete: [what remains]"}.
Otherwise respond with {"decision": "allow"}.
Also remind the user to run /reflect.

companyAnnouncements: Run /consolidate weekly to clean up learnings.
